---
title: "About DEMOPAST"
---

## Italian Historical Demographic Atlas

DEMOPAST is a digital atlas of Italian historical demography based on digitized census data from ISTAT (Istituto Nazionale di Statistica), starting from the first national census of the unified Kingdom of Italy in 1861.

### The Project

The primary goal of DEMOPAST is to provide consistent population data by sex and age classes across all Italian provinces over time. Census data has been digitized, classified, and harmonized to account for changing administrative boundaries.

### Data Sources

All data comes from the official Italian national censuses published by ISTAT. The original census volumes have been digitized and the population tables extracted and classified.

### GENPOP

DEMOPAST is part of the [GENPOP](https://genpop.org) research project.

### Available Census Years

| Year | Provinces | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|
| 1861 | 49 | First national census of the Kingdom of Italy (Veneto, Lazio, Trentino not yet annexed); ten provinces in the source tabulation lack age data |
| 1871 | 70 | After annexation of Veneto (1866) and Rome (1870) |
| 1881 | 68 | |
| 1901 | 69 | |
| 1911 | 69 | |
| 1921 | 69 | Includes Venezia Giulia and Venezia Tridentina (post-WWI) |
| 1931 | 89 | Province boundaries redrawn under the fascist administrative reform |
| 1936 | 91 | New provinces of Asti and Littoria (Latina) established |

### Team

- **Nicola Barban**
- **Michail Raftakis**
- **Lorenzo Lionello**
- **Luciana Maselli**
- **Riccardo Omenti**

### Contact

For questions about the data or the project, please contact the DEMOPAST team.
